Package org.efs.timer

package org.efs.timer
efs timer package provides EfsScheduledExecutor which encapsulates a ScheduledExecutorService. The idea is that EfsScheduledExecutor translates timer expirations into timer events dispatched to an efs agent.

The reason for this efs scheduled executor is to make it possible for efs agents to process timer events solely within the agent's dispatcher. This means agents remain effectively single threaded. If ScheduledExecutorService were used to directly access an efs agent, then the efs agent is no longer singled threaded and thread safety measures are needed.

Note that the user-provided ScheduledExecutorService may be used independently of the EfsScheduledExecutor to execute other tasks and even be shut down outside of the efs scheduled executor.

Scheduling Timers

EfsScheduledExecutor provides timer scheduling methods similar to ScheduledExecutorService:

  • EfScheduledExecutor.schedule(String timerName, Consumer<EfsTimerEvent> callback, IEfsAgent agent, Duration delay)
  • EfsScheduledExecutor.scheduleAtFixedRate(String timerName, Consumer<EfsTimerEvent> callback, IEfsAgent agent, Duration initialDelay, Duration period)
  • EfsScheduledExecutor.scheduleWithFixedDelay(String timerName, Consumer<EfsTimerEvent> callback, IEfsAgent agent, Duration initialDelay, Duration delay)

The main differences between Java and efs schedulers are:

  • efs requires IEfsAgent and Consumer<EfsTimerEvent> arguments and does not support scheduling Callable or Runnable commands. Note: the provided efs agent must be registered with an efs dispatcher prior to using that agent to schedule a timer.
  • EfsScheduledExecutor uses Duration to specify delay/period values rather than a long and TimeUnit.

Creating efs Scheduled Executors

Creating an efs scheduled executor is as simple as:

  1. Creating a ScheduledExecutorService instance and
  2. passing that ScheduledExecutorService instance to the efs scheduled executor constructor EfsScheduleExecutor(ScheduledExecutorService).
  • Classes
    Class
    Description
    EfsScheduledExecutor
    Executes given timer, IEfsAgent pairs at a specified time.
    EfsTimerEvent
    Event reporting a timer expiration.
